pr_description:
Fixes #3453 #3559.

### Motivation

`schema.Invalidate` only supported single entity types, making it impossible to use with polymorphic delete endpoints that can remove different entity types based on the response. This is a common pattern when APIs return `{ id, type }` for delete operations where `type` determines which entity was deleted.

### Solution

Rewrote `Invalidate` to extend `PolymorphicSchema`, enabling unified handling of both single entities and Union schemas with minimal code duplication.

**New usage patterns:**

```typescript
// With existing Union schema (recommended)
const MemberUnion = new schema.Union({ users: User, groups: Group }, 'type');
new schema.Invalidate(MemberUnion);

// Inline polymorphic definition
new schema.Invalidate({ users: User, groups: Group }, 'type');

// Function schemaAttribute for complex discrimination
new schema.Invalidate(
  { users: User, groups: Group },
  (input) => input.memberType === 'user' ? 'users' : 'groups'
);
```

**Key technical decisions:**

- Extends `PolymorphicSchema` to reuse `inferSchema()`, `denormalizeValue()`, and `schemaKey()` methods
- Properly calls `process()` before `pk()` to handle entities with computed primary keys
- Handles both object and primitive (string/number) inputs for delete responses that return just an id
- Falls back to returning the id when entity not found during denormalization (common for delete operations)

### Changes

- `packages/endpoint/src/schemas/Invalidate.ts` - Rewrote to extend PolymorphicSchema
- `docs/rest/api/Invalidate.md` - Added polymorphic types documentation
- Added tests for Union normalization/denormalization
- Added controller fetch tests for delete operations with object and string responses

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Adds polymorphic delete support and refactors invalidation logic.
> 
> - Rewrites `packages/endpoint/src/schemas/Invalidate.ts` to extend `PolymorphicSchema`, accepting `Union` or `{ key: Entity }` with `schemaAttribute`; handles id-only inputs, calls `delegate.invalidate`, and returns pk or `{ id, schema }`
> - Refines polymorphic hoisting: `Union` is now hoistable (`_hoistable = true`) while others (e.g., `Array`, `Values`, `Invalidate`) do not hoist; updates `Polymorphic.define()` accordingly
> - Documents polymorphic usage in `docs/rest/api/Invalidate.md` with examples for Union/string/function `schemaAttribute`
> - Expands tests: Union normalization/denormalization for `Invalidate`, nested polymorphic behavior in `Array`/`Values`, and React controller fetch delete cases (entity vs id-only)
> - Adds changeset and release blog entry noting "Invalidate supports Unions"
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 4cde0b711c76111d990a265cb750042d92cb368d. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

commit_message: feat: Invalidate accepts Unions

pr_description:
### Motivation

When using polymorphic entities via [Union](https://dataclient.io/rest/api/Union) schema (e.g., a feed containing posts, comments, and other item types), deleting items was cumbersome. Users had to manually wrap Union schemas in `schema.Invalidate` even when using `resource()`, which already handles this automatically for Entity schemas.

### Solution

**1. Enhanced `schema.Invalidate` types to accept Union schemas directly**

The `Invalidate` constructor now accepts a `Union` instance in addition to entities and entity maps:

new schema.Invalidate(MyUnionSchema)**2. Updated `resource().delete` to auto-detect Union schemas**

`resource()` now checks for the `_hoistable` marker on Union schemas and automatically wraps them with `Invalidate`, matching the existing behavior for entities:

```ts
const FeedResource = resource({
  path: '/feed/:id',
  schema: FeedUnion,
});
// FeedResource.delete now uses Invalidate(FeedUnion) automatically
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[resource.schema] --> B{Has .process?}
    B -->|Entity| C[Invalidate wrapper]
    B -->|No| D{Has ._hoistable?}
    D -->|Union| C
    D -->|No| E[Use as-is]
```

### Open questions

None - this is a straightforward extension of existing patterns.
commit_message: enhance: Invalidate() types should allow for Union arg

pr_description:
### Motivation Moving entities between filtered collections (e.g., kanban columns, group reassignment, status transitions) currently requires separate `remove` + `push` calls. A single atomic `move` operation is more ergonomic and ensures both the removal and addition happen in one normalization pass, preventing UI flicker. ### Solution Add `Collection.move` schema and `RestEndpoint.move` extender that atomically removes an entity from collections matching its **existing** state and adds it to collections matching the **new** state (derived from the body/last arg). ```mermaid flowchart LR A[PATCH /tasks/3
body: status=in-progress] --> B[normalizeMove] B --> C[Lookup existing entity
status=backlog] B --> D[Merge: existing + body
status=in-progress] C --> E[removeFilter → backlog collection] D --> F[addFilter → in-progress collection] ``` Key design decisions: - **Last-arg convention**: The last argument (request body for REST) determines the new collection membership, matching standard PATCH semantics where path params identify the resource and the body carries the update - **`movePath` option**: `resource()` passes the full path (before shortening) so `.move` can use path params for collection filtering, while `getList` uses the shortened path - **`MoveEndpoint` type**: Separate from `AddEndpoint`/`RemoveEndpoint` — strips `searchParams` from the type since `.move` uses the full entity path, and uses `PATCH` method - **Reuses `createCollectionFilter`**: Same matching logic as push/remove, making behavior predictable and allowing customization via the existing override point Works for both `Collection(Array)` and `Collection(Values)`. ```ts // Path param filtering const UserResource = resource({ path: '/groups/:group/users/:id', schema: User, }); await ctrl.fetch(UserResource.getList.move, { group: 'five', id: '2' }, { id: '2', group: 'ten' }); // Search param filtering const TaskResource = resource({ path: '/tasks/:id', searchParams: {} as { status: string }, schema: Task, }); await ctrl.fetch(TaskResource.getList.move, { id: '3' }, { id: '3', status: 'in-progress' }); ``` --- > [!NOTE] > **Medium Risk** > Touches core normalization/collection merge behavior and adds new PATCH mutation semantics, which could affect list membership updates and optimistic flows across the app. Risk is mitigated by extensive new unit/integration tests covering Array/Values collections, multi-arg paths, and optimistic + FormData bodies. > > **Overview** > Introduces `Collection.move` (and extends `Collection.remove` to work for `Values`) to atomically remove entities from collections matching their **existing** store state and add them to collections matching the **new** values derived from the last arg/body, including per-entity handling for multi-item inputs. > > Adds `RestEndpoint.move`/`Resource.getList.move` as a `PATCH` extender that targets the full entity path via a new `movePath` (dropping `searchParams`), updates TypeScript types accordingly, and ensures optimistic action args/bodies are POJOs so `FormData` works. > > Expands tests for move behavior (Array + Values, path/search-param keyed collections, missing entity fields, nested schemas, optimistic updates) and updates docs/blog/examples plus playground fixtures/styles to document and demo `move`. > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 820c174bd0ba8e5e715fd3f8e25f22ff01f92dfe. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).
commit_message: feat: Add Collection.move + RestEndpoint.move

pr_description:
### Motivation

path-to-regexp v8 changed repeating/wildcard params (`*name`) to require array values instead of strings. The existing type system and runtime code in `@data-client/rest` did not account for this — wildcards were typed as `string | number` and the runtime `url()` method stringified all values, breaking array params.

### Solution

**Types** (`pathTypes.ts`): Simplified from 14 to 11 type helpers by unifying `ParamSplits`/`WildcardSplits` into a single `Splits<S, M>` generic. Wildcard keys are tagged with `*` prefix internally, which `KeyVal` uses to determine `string[]` vs `string | number`, and `KeyName` strips for the final property name. Correctly handles required vs optional wildcards, quoted params, escaped characters, and adjacency edge cases.

**Runtime** (`RestEndpoint.js`): `url()` now preserves array values for wildcard params instead of calling `String()` on them, passing them through to `compile()` as `path-to-regexp` v8 expects.

**Tests**: Added 4 runtime tests for wildcard URL generation (required, mixed with params, optional, optional with required param). Updated type tests with comprehensive wildcard/v8 assertions (214 type assertions in `pathTypes.test.ts`, 182 new lines in `types.test.ts`).

### Open questions

N/A


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core URL construction and type inference for `RestEndpoint.path`/`resource()` paths, which can break existing wildcard usage and path-templating edge cases. Risk is mitigated by expanded runtime and TypeScript test coverage.
> 
> **Overview**
> Aligns `RestEndpoint.path`/`Resource.path` with `path-to-regexp` v8 semantics, notably making `*wildcard` path params compile from `string[]` (including optional wildcards) and updating `shortenPath()` to strip the last `:param` *or* `*wildcard` when deriving list/create paths.
> 
> Updates the path type-level parser (`PathKeys`/`PathArgs`/`PathArgsAndSearch`) to correctly infer wildcard arrays, optional groups, quoted names, escapes, and adjacency edge cases, and adds/updates runtime + TS tests plus migration/docs notes (changeset, REST docs, and release blog) to reflect the new v8 syntax and escaping rules.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 9a8bfe063bb926dbbf3ef7945fe79e12d01fe255. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

### Motivation

-->
In https://github.com/coinbase/rest-hooks/pull/360 we solve an infinite loop problem when the network response was invalid. This required detecting and throwing errors before suspending so it doesn't just keep suspending infinitely.

pr_description:
In other words: don't extraneously refetch fresh data.

BREAKING CHANGE: useResource() inferred endpoint will sometimes
not trigger a fetch if entities are fresh enough

Fixes #460 .
Also requested by: @melissafzhang @dgcoffman 
Continuation of: #439

### Motivation
<!--
Does this solve a bug? Enable a new use-case? Improve an existing behavior? Concrete examples are helpful here.
-->
Allows liberal use of useResource()

- When a detail (inferrable) useResouce() is in a nested component of a list result, will not trigger `N` fetches
- Any other case where manual useCache() tracking was used.

### Solution
<!--
What is the solution here from a high level. What are the key technical decisions and why were they made?
-->
- Previously inferred results were set to expiry time of 0, which meant they would try to fetch if they could
- Now this is computed based on the 'oldest' (or soonest to expire) entity of those used.
- If any entities are missing, the full result was not inferred and thus expiry is 0.
- Also went back to previous denormalize() design (from previous commit) that returns the localCache of entities. However, this time we have a slightly cleaner interface to getUnvisit().
- entitiesExpireAt added to several hooks `useRetrieve()`, `useExpiresAt()`. This defaults to 0 so previous behavior is maintained.
- entitiesExpireAt is now returned from `useDenormalized()`

pr_description:
Previously failing or deleting results will
now work:
- Arrays no longer delete items where members are missing
- Results missing members will return what they have

Also:
- Results in cache are always 'ready', and thus ignore found
- We only infer results, and not entities, thus entities' nested members are always 'found'

Fixes #637.

### Motivation
<!--
Does this solve a bug? Enable a new use-case? Improve an existing behavior? Concrete examples are helpful here.
-->
Requiring explicit marking of optional is cumbersome.

Principal: easy adoption, incremental safety

### Solution
<!--
What is the solution here from a high level. What are the key technical decisions and why were they made?
-->
Previously hard coding required into these paths was thought to be necessary so inferred results wouldn't prematurely return results. However, it is easy to detect when this is the case - enabling us to be more lax when actual results exist. Furthermore, with deletes marked explicitly we don't expect any internal state problems.

Changes:
- Arrays filter only based on deleted, or if the value is undefined
- Full results from useCache and useResource check if inferred to determine whether they care about 'found'
- Entities' nested found is always true

Testing:
- Just commented out tests for 'required' fields, as these will be useful to modify when we have opt-in required
- Infinite loop case with synthetic errors is no longer triggerable due to results cache ignoring 'found' result.
- New test module based on https://github.com/coinbase/rest-hooks/issues/637 example with a few additions

### Next Steps

Opt-in to stricter requirements

```typescript
export class SomeResource extends Resource {
  id: string = "";
  things: Nested[] = [];
  requiredThings: Nested[] = [];
  requiredPrimitive: number = 0;

  pk() {
    return this.id;
  }

  static schema = {
    things: [Nested],
    // [] is just shorthand for Array(), but using the longer version lets us define additional options
    requiredThings: new schema.Array(Nested, { required: true }),
    // introduce a new schema that *only* is used for validation (no data transforms)
    requiredPrimitive: new schema.Required(0),
  };

  static urlRoot = "/some";
}
```

### Breaking change? Semver?

This is technically a breaking behavior. However,

- It is *relaxing* failures. Therefore it's hard to imagine existing working cases failing.
  - *not* changing behavior from one case to another
  - *not* restricting powers, making new cases fail
- Is a hurdle for those upgrading to 5.

Therefore, we are pragmatically not doing a semver bump.
commit_message:
enhance: All schema members are optional by default

Previously failing or deleting results will
now work:
- Arrays no longer delete items where members are missing
- Results missing members will return what they have

Inferred results still must require all fields by default, so
responses with optional members will not be inferred when
they are not present.

pr_description:
### Motivation
<!--
Does this solve a bug? Enable a new use-case? Improve an existing behavior? Concrete examples are helpful here.
-->
- Simplifies serialization of normalized values
- Improve performance

### Solution
<!--
What is the solution here from a high level. What are the key technical decisions and why were they made?
-->

We introduce these new designs into the experimental packages to flesh out before planning migration.

#### Entity
- Normalizes to pojos
  - new: `static process()` (instead of fromJS())
- Does not include Record concepts
  - This can be a performance benefit for those who aren't interested in the helper methods that track which members are defined
  - merge & fromJS are both a lot faster. These operations are performance for every entity in a response.

#### EntityRecord
- Implements the same interface as prior Entity to provide backcompat as well as utility
- Implementation has some overhead but much less than SimpleRecord
- For most this will be 100% backwards compatible as the only distinction is the normalize to pojo, which is uncommon to rely on

pr_description:
### Motivation
<!--
Does this solve a bug? Enable a new use-case? Improve an existing behavior? Concrete examples are helpful here.
-->
A place for old behavior to exist so code can move on.

### Solution
<!--
What is the solution here from a high level. What are the key technical decisions and why were they made?
-->
Move Entity+Resource chain to legacy. rest-hooks simply re-exports which doesn't change its behavior.
In a future release we can remove the export and leave legacy. This makes it easy to incrementally migrate.

Note: All files are simply moved and some book-keeping with package.json and tsconfig are the only actual file changes.
commit_message: feat: Add Resource+Entity to legacy
